result.reason: Workflow orchestration script with data transformation logic in the report phase and agent coordination; moderate complexity but not core business logic

suggestions.description: Test the compile-report phase logic: verify files are correctly grouped by priority (critical/high/medium/low/skip) when given mock analysis results
Test fallback handling when analysis result is null or non-object - should default to {priority:'medium', needs_unit:false, needs_integration:false, reason:'analysis failed'}
Test summary statistics calculation: needs_unit_tests count, needs_integration_tests count, already_has_tests count, and total_test_suggestions sum across all results
Test default args resolution: dir defaults to '.', extensions defaults to 'ts,js,tsx,jsx,py,go,rs', exclude defaults to expected directories
Test early return with error object when discovery returns empty array or non-array value

Test full workflow execution with mocked agent/pipeline/step/log dependencies - verify agent is called with correct prompts and schemas, pipeline processes all files, and final report structure is correct
Test that the discovery phase agent prompt correctly incorporates custom args.dir, args.extensions, and args.exclude values
Test meta export has required fields: name, description, and phases array with correct structure (title and detail for each phase)

risk_factors: Report phase grouping logic could silently produce wrong priority counts if filter condition has a typo
Fallback when analysis fails could mask real errors - no logging of which files failed
Summary statistics could be wrong if optional chaining on suggestions?.length misses edge cases
Custom args override could break if args is undefined vs null (only checks args?.dir)

result.reason: Main extension entry point with complex action-routing logic, error handling, and state management in the tool execute handler; only formatRun is currently tested
risk_factors: Action routing bugs (wrong branch for list/status/cancel/start) could break the entire tool
Cancel action modifies step statuses in a loop - off-by-one or wrong status filter could leave runs in inconsistent state
Invalid JSON args not caught properly could crash the extension
Start action has multiple failure modes (workflow not found, no default export, execution error) that need distinct handling
Race condition in step tracking - saveRun called in onStep callback could lose updates if steps complete rapidly
Session_start event handlers silently swallow errors which could hide workflow discovery failures

suggestions.description: Test 'list' action returns formatted workflow list with names, sources, dirs, and recent runs from store
Test 'status' action returns error when run_id is missing, returns 'not found' for invalid run_id, and returns formatted run for valid run_id
Test 'cancel' action sets run status to 'cancelled', updates timestamps, and transitions only pending/running steps to 'cancelled' (leaving completed/failed steps unchanged)
Test 'start' action with unknown workflow name returns helpful error with available workflows list and template
Test 'start' action rejects invalid JSON in params.args with clear error message
Test 'start' action with a workflow that has no default export marks run as completed and returns informative message
Test 'start' action executes workflow default function, passes parsed args via runtime, saves run with 'completed' status and result on success
Test 'start' action catches execution errors, saves run with 'failed' status, and returns error message
Test step tracking callback during workflow execution: new steps are pushed, existing steps (same name+startedAt) are updated in place, and run is persisted after each change
Test command handler with no args or 'list' calls listWorkflows and shows select UI, then sends user message with selected workflow name
Test command handler with workflow name and extra args sends user message including both name and args string
Test session_start event handler sets UI status with correct workflow count when workflows exist, and does nothing when none found

result.reason: Core workflow discovery/loading logic with filesystem traversal, path resolution, and deduplication - bugs here break all workflow functionality
risk_factors: Incorrect git root detection could cause workflows to be missed or discovered from wrong directories
Directory traversal logic could infinite-loop or skip parent dirs on edge cases (symlinks, root filesystems)
Workflow deduplication (project overrides global) could break if ordering changes
loadWorkflow silently returns null on modules without meta.name - could mask real import errors
Extension matching is case-sensitive via toLowerCase but real filesystems may behave differently
listDir swallows all errors uniformly - permission errors look the same as missing directories

suggestions.description: Test getProjectWorkflowDir returns correct path combining cwd with .pi/workflows
Test findGitRoot traverses up from nested directory to find .git directory and returns that path
Test findGitRoot returns null when no .git exists (reaches filesystem root)
Test discoverProjectDirs generates all PROJECT_DIR_NAMES variants for each directory from cwd up to git root
Test discoverProjectDirs stops at git root and doesn't traverse above it
Test getAllWorkflowDirs returns project dirs followed by global dirs in correct order
Test listDir filters files by EXTENSIONS (.js, .ts, .mjs, .mts) and ignores other files like .json or .md
Test listDir returns empty array for non-existent directory (graceful error handling)
Test listWorkflows deduplicates by name - project workflows shadow global workflows with same name
Test listWorkflows returns results sorted alphabetically by name
Test loadWorkflow tries all extensions in order and returns first successful import with valid meta
Test loadWorkflow returns null when module exists but lacks meta.name property
Test loadWorkflow returns null when no matching workflow file exists in any directory
Test full discovery with a temp directory structure: project workflow overrides global workflow of same name

result.reason: Good existing test coverage for pipeline/agent/log, but the `step` function is completely untested and `createEmitResultTool` non-object schema wrapping path lacks coverage
risk_factors: step() function has no tests - status tracking and error re-throw behavior unverified
Non-object schema wrapping/unwrapping in createEmitResultTool could silently break structured output for array/primitive schemas
Agent with schema where emit_result tool is successfully called (captured result path) is only tested via fallback path
Pipeline error logging message format could regress without notice

suggestions.description: Test step() tracks running -> completed status and returns the function result
Test step() tracks running -> failed status and re-throws the error (unlike agent which swallows errors)
Test step() sets startedAt and completedAt timestamps correctly
Test agent with non-object schema (e.g. type:'array') wraps prompt with 'Pass your result in the result parameter' instruction
Test createEmitResultTool with non-object schema wraps in {type:'object', properties:{result:schema}} and unwraps .result from params on execute
Test createEmitResultTool execute() returns terminate:true and 'Result captured.' text
Test agent with schema where getResult() returns a value (simulate tool execute being called) - verifies step.result contains captured structured data
Test agent returns undefined and sets step.error when emit_result tool was never called (current fallback path already partially tested but error message not asserted)

result.reason: Comprehensive tests already exist covering all exported functions, error paths, edge cases, and sorting logic
risk_factors: Concurrent writes could corrupt run files (not currently tested)
Special characters in runId could produce invalid file paths

suggestions.description: Test concurrent saveRun calls for the same runId to verify last-write-wins behavior without data corruption
Test that runId values with path separators or special chars (e.g., '../escape', 'run/bad') don't allow path traversal

result.reason: Pure type/interface definitions file with no runtime logic, no functions, no classes — only TypeScript type declarations and interfaces.
risk_factors: Type definitions could become inconsistent with runtime implementations, but this is caught by the TypeScript compiler, not tests
